nodes.context: English crime gang based in South London in the 1960s, involved in racketeering, extortion, torture, and drug trafficking.
Notorious East London gang led by the Kray twins, involved in organized crime and turf wars.
East End gang that George Cornell originally belonged to before joining the Richardsons.
Manchester-based criminal group; Dougie Flood was an alleged member.

nodes.original_text_name: South London gang led by Billy Hayward
nodes.context: South London gang involved in the brawl at Mr Smith's Club.

edges.context: The Richardson Gang and the Kray twins were engaged in a turf war in the mid-to-late 1960s.
George Cornell, originally a member of the Watney Streeters, later allied himself with the Richardsons.
George Cornell was originally involved with the Krays before switching sides.
Dougie Flood, an alleged member of the Quality Street Gang, asked Eddie Richardson and Frankie Fraser to protect Mr Smith's Club.
Members of the Hayward Gang were involved in a brawl with the Richardson Gang at Mr Smith's Club.
